LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO SEP-04-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT INTEL CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF VINAY K. AWASTHI BRINGS THIS ACTION CHALLENGING HIS DISCHARGE FROM INTEL CORPORATION. THE COMPLAINT ALSO ALLEGES THAT HE WAS DRUGGED AND SURVEILLED. INTEL DEMURS. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, HEARING RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE RECONSIDERATION AND STAY OF ITS PRIOR DEMURRER ORDER OF NOV-17-2025. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DISCHARGES ITS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND DOES NOT ENTER A STAY. THE CASE IS NOW ON APPEAL AND THE AUTOMATIC APPELLATE STAY APPLIES. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER, IN COMPLIANCE WITH C.R.C. 3.1312, WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, FUTHER HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. PLAINTIFF VINAY K. AWASTHI HAS RESTORED JURISDICTION TO THIS COURT BY FILING A NOTICE OF ABANDONMENT OF HIS APPEAL. FOR THE REASONS STATED IN THE COURT'S OCTOBER 3 ORDER, THE COURT SUA SPONTE RECONSIDERS ITS ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT INTEL CORPORATION'S DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT NOW SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. IN THIS ACTION, AWASTHI MAY NOT RELITIGATE MATTERS THAT WERE DECIDED IN CASE NO. 24-CV-05621-WHO (N.D. CAL.). BUT HE MAY AMEND TO ALLEGE CLAIMS THAT WERE NOT DECIDED IN THAT ACTION, AND TO ADDRESS OTHER ISSUES RAISED IN INTEL'S DEMURRER. AWASTHI SHALL FILE HIS AMENDED COMPLAINT BY DECEMBER 15, 2025. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT INTEL CORPORATION, AS PREVAILING PARTY, SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: ANASTASSIA KOKEZAS CSR# 14320 510-258-9855 ANASTASSIA@AOL.COM REPORTED (301/CVA)

FUTHER HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION
Off Calendar Nov-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Vinay K. Awasthi, 415-500-5913 vajhu@proton.me Pro Per Plaintiff.

Regan Heslop, Esq., JACKSON LEWIS P.C. (415) 394-9400 Regan.Heslop@jacksonlewis.com Attorney for Defendant INTEL CORPORATION

Anastassia Kokezas CSR# 14320 510-258-9855 anastassia@aol.com Court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

FUTHER HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION.

Plaintiff Vinay K. Awasthi has restored jurisdiction to this court by filing a notice of abandonment of his appeal. For the reasons stated in the court's October 3 order, the court sua sponte reconsiders its order sustaining defendant Intel Corporation's demurrer without leave to amend. The court now sustains the demurrer with leave to amend. In this action, Awasthi may not relitigate matters that were decided in Case No. 24-cv-05621-WHO (N.D. Cal.). But he may amend to allege claims that were not decided in that action, and to address other issues raised in Intel's demurrer. Awasthi shall file his amended complaint by December 15, 2025.

Counsel for Defendant Intel Corporation, as prevailing party, shall submit a proposed order verbatim to the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Anastassia Kokezas CSR# 14320 510-258-9855 anastassia@aol.com Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT INTEL CORPORATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Vinay K. Awasthi brings this action challenging his discharge from Intel Corporation. The complaint also alleges that he was drugged and surveilled. Intel demurs. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend.

The court grants the request for judicial notice of documents from the federal lawsuit, Case No. 24-cv-05621-WHO (N.D. Cal.). In his federal complaint, Awasthi challenged his termination and alleged that he was surveilled and drugged. While the federal case was pending, Awasthi filed the present action. Intel removed it to federal court and the federal district court related the actions. (RJN, Ex. L.) The federal court dismissed his federal lawsuit with prejudice and remanded this case. (Id., Ex. L., p. 7.)

Intel bases its demurrer on a variety of reasons. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend because the action is barred by claim preclusion. Awasthi's federal complaint raised the same issues and the same invasion of primary rights (his interest in his employment at Intel and the bodily integrity and privacy issues raised in both proceedings). Intel obtained a final judgment on the merits. Awasthi may not now relitigate the same claims. (See Mycogen Corp. v. Monsanto Co. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 888, 896-97; Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 793.) Because this ground is sufficient, the court does not reach Intel's remaining arguments.

The court must grant leave to amend unless it appears that amendment would be futile. Given the diverse nature of the claims raised in the federal case, it appears that amendment would be futile here.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
